docs.title: Developer Platform Overview
docs.text:
Welcome to the Documentation Automation MVP!

This site demonstrates the power of Docs as Code. It automatically extracts docstrings directly from the Python (FastAPI) backend and serves them here dynamically.

docs.title: Features Included
docs.text:
• FastAPI Backend with Google-style docstrings
  • Next.js Client application (Frontend)
  • Custom AST Parser for Architecture Diagram generation
  • MkDocs configuration using material theme
  • API block injections utilizing mkdocstrings

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Navigation
docs.text:
• API Reference - This is where your code is documented!
  • Architecture Overview - See how modules connect.
  • Installation Guide - Get up and running.
  • Tutorials - Learn with notebooks.
  • Navigation Test - Verify the system!
  • Sphinx Version - Toggle to the Sphinx representation of this documentation.

docs.title: Working Locally
docs.text:
To see the live docs when changing code:

python scripts/run_pipeline.py

The site will be available at: http://127.0.0.1:8001/AutoCodeDoc/

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Below is the auto-generated architecture tree reflecting the current state of our FastAPI backend application.

This generation occurs locally and updates based purely on the directory structure of our backend/app folder.

FastAPI Application Architecture Tree
=====================================

├── API Routes
│    ├── auth
│    ├── billing
│    ├── inventory
│    ├── notifications
│    ├── organizations
│    ├── payments
│    ├── users
│    ├── vehicles
│
├── Service Layer
│    ├── billing_service
│    ├── notification_service
│    ├── org_service
│    ├── payment_service
│    ├── user_service
│    ├── vehicle_service
│
├── Data Models
     ├── auth
     ├── invoice
     ├── notification
     ├── organization
     ├── payment
     ├── stock
     ├── user
     ├── vehicle
│
├── Utilities
     ├── formatting
     ├── validation

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to the AutoCodeDoc Platform will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Sphinx + AutoAPI as primary documentation engine
  • Fully automated API reference generation via sphinx-autoapi
  • NumPy-style docstring support via Napoleon
  • Mermaid.js diagram rendering via sphinxcontrib-mermaid
  • Cross-repo intersphinx linking

  • Responsive RTD theme with custom CSS

  • Architecture Diagrams (auto-generated)

  • System architecture from Python import graph (AST-based)
  • ER diagrams from Pydantic model introspection

  • Cross-repo data flow (mllam-data-prep → weather-model-graphs → neural-lam)

  • ML/LLM Documentation Sections

  • Model architecture visualization
  • Training data pipeline documentation
  • Hyperparameter schema extraction

  • Evaluation metrics structure

  • Quality & CI/CD

  • interrogate integration for non-blocking docstring coverage
  • Unified run_pipeline.py for single-command builds
  • docs_report.json feedback artifact after every build

  • GitHub Actions workflow with lint → coverage → build → deploy

  • Backend Test Suite

  • test_docs_build.py — Sphinx build validation
  • test_coverage.py — Docstring coverage via interrogate
  • test_diagrams.py — Diagram output validation

  • test_autoapi.py — AutoAPI index verification

  • Static Content Pages

  • Installation guide with environment setup
  • End-to-end usage walkthrough
  • Contributing guide with NumPy docstring templates
  • This changelog

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• MkDocs demoted to secondary documentation at /legacy-docs/
  • Napoleon config switched from Google-style to NumPy-style
  • requirements.txt updated with all Sphinx and quality tooling

docs.title: Preserved
docs.text:
• Full MkDocs site functionality (additive change only)
  • All existing documented items (endpoints, schemas, services, utilities)
  • GitHub Pages deployment via peaceiris/actions-gh-pages

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial MkDocs + Material theme setup
  • Custom AST-based architecture diagram generation
  • Pydantic field description integrity checker
  • mkdocstrings for API reference from Google-style docstrings
  • Basic Sphinx setup in docs_sphinx/ with sphinx-rtd-theme
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD for MkDocs deployment

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
We welcome contributions! Please follow these steps:

  1. Fork the repo.
  2. Create your feature branch (git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature).
  3. Commit your changes (git commit -m 'Add some AmazingFeature').
  4. Push to the branch (git push origin feature/AmazingFeature).
  5. Open a Pull Request.

docs.title: Code Standards
docs.text:
• Follow PEP 8 for Python.
  • Include Google-style docstrings for all new functions.
  • Ensure all tests pass.

docs.title: Database Schema
docs.text: Detailed breakdown of the platform's data models.

docs.title: Entity: Address
docs.text:
Physical address of a user.

Field Type Description Default street str Street name and number. Required city str City name. Required zip_code str Postal zip code. Required

docs.title: Entity: Product
docs.text:
Model representing a store product.

Field Type Description Default id int Unique product ID. Required name str Name of the product. Required price float Price in USD. Required category str Product category (e.g., 'Electronics'). Required

docs.title: Entity: User
docs.text:
User model representing an account in the system.

Field Type Description Default id int The unique identifier for the user. Required username str The login username. Required email str The email address of the user. Required role str The user's role (e.g., 'admin', 'user'). user bio Optional A short biography of the user. None is_active bool Whether the user account is currently active. True last_login Optional Timestamp of the last successful login. None tags List Categorization tags for the user. list() address Optional User's primary mailing address. None phone_number Optional The user's contact phone number. None preferred_language str The user's preferred language code. en secret_note Optional A private note for administrative use only. None

docs.title: Entity: UserCreate
docs.text:
Model used to create a new User entry.

Field Type Description Default username str The chosen username. Required email str The associated email address. Required bio Optional A short biography for the profile. None

docs.title: Entity: PaymentRequest
docs.text:
Model used to initiate a payment transaction.

Field Type Description Default amount float The transaction amount. Required currency str The currency code (e.g., 'USD'). USD payment_method str The chosen payment method (e.g., 'credit_card', 'paypal'). credit_card

docs.title: Entity: PaymentResponse
docs.text:
Response returned after a payment attempt.

Field Type Description Default transaction_id str Unique gateway transaction ID. Required status str Payment status (e.g., 'success', 'failed'). Required

docs.title: Entity: Token
docs.text:
Authentication token details.

Field Type Description Default access_token str The JWT access token. Required token_type str The type of token (e.g., 'Bearer'). Required expires_in int The duration in seconds until the token expires. 3600

docs.title: Data Relationships
docs.text:
erDiagram
    Address {
        str street
        str city
        str zip_code
    }
    Product {
        int id
        str name
        float price
        str category
    }
    User {
        int id
        str username
        str email
        str role
        Optional bio
        bool is_active
        Optional last_login
        List tags
        Optional address
        Optional phone_number
        str preferred_language
        Optional secret_note
    }
    User |o--|| Address : "references"
    UserCreate {
        str username
        str email
        Optional bio
    }

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: Welcome to the installation documentation. You can run this platform in multiple environments.

docs.title: Local Environment (Direct)
docs.text:
To install this documentation automation platform locally on Windows:

  1. Clone the repository: 
    git clone https://github.com/example/doc_automation_platform.git
  2. Set up the Python Virtual Environment: 
    cd doc_automation_platform/backend
python -m venv venv
.\venv\Scripts\activate
  3. Install dependencies: 
    pip install -r requirements.txt
  4. Run the documentation server: 
    python scripts/run_pipeline.py
  5. View the docs at: http://127.0.0.1:8001/AutoCodeDoc/

docs.title: Production Environment
docs.text: We currently advise using Docker to serve the MkDocs site in a containerized format, which we will document in a future sprint.

docs.title: Media and Assets
docs.text: This section documents the visual assets used in the project.

docs.title: Logo
docs.text: The logo is stored in assets/logo.png.

docs.title: Diagrams
docs.text: Most diagrams are auto-generated. See the Architecture section for the latest system flow.

docs.title: 🚀 Documentation Automation & Verification
docs.text: This guide explains how to ensure your documentation stays in sync with your code and how to verify it.

docs.title: 🔄 Automatic Updates
docs.text: The documentation is configured to automatically update every time you build or serve the site. This is handled by the gen-files plugin in mkdocs.yml, which triggers: - scripts/gen_ref_pages.py: Generates the Python API reference. - scripts/gen_architecture.py: Updates the system architecture tree.

docs.title: The "One Command" to Rule Them All
docs.text:
Run this from the project root (doc_automation_platform) to start a live-reloading server:

python scripts/run_pipeline.py

[!TIP] Live Updates: While the server is running, any changes you make to .py files in backend/app/ or .md files in docs/ will be reflected immediately in the browser.

docs.title: 🚀 Unified Pipeline (CI/CD Ready)
docs.text:
The easiest way to check everything is to run the master pipeline script. This is what you would use in GitHub Actions:

# Run integrity checks + build + serve
.\venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/run_pipeline.py --serve

# Build only (for CI/CD)
.\venv\Scripts/python.exe scripts/run_pipeline.py --build-only

docs.title: 🧪 Verification Checklist
docs.text:
1. Start the server using the command above.
  2. Open http://127.0.0.1:8001/AutoCodeDoc/ in your browser.
  3. Check System Design: Click Design > Overview to see the architecture tree.
  4. Check API Docs: Click API Reference > API > app > api > auth to verify the code reference.
  5. Verify No 404s: Every link in the sidebar should now resolve correctly.
  6. Documentation Integrity
    • Run .\venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/check_doc_sync.py
    • Verify it returns "All fields are correctly documented!"

docs.title: 🛠️ Troubleshooting
docs.text: If you don't see your changes: - Check the terminal for any Python errors in the generation scripts. - Ensure you have saved your code changes. - Restart the mkdocs serve command.

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This project automatically generates API documentation by reading the docstrings inside your FastAPI components (backend/app/api, backend/app/services, and backend/app/models).

docs.title: Adding a new API Endpoint
docs.text:
When you create a new Python module, doc_automation_platform dynamically renders it! For example:

  1. Create backend/app/api/new_feature.py.
  2. Add a basic FastAPI router, and provide a single Google-style Docstring string under your function definition.
  3. Once saved, mkdocs-gen-files will pick it up automatically during mkdocs serve or mkdocs build.

The system also auto-links nested models to their appropriate service schemas.

docs.title: Module Index
docs.text: Select a module from the sidebar to view its documentation.

docs.title: read_root()
docs.text:
Root endpoint for the Developer Platform API.

Returns:

Name Type Description dict dict

A simple welcome message and API status.

docs.title: login(username, password) async
docs.text:
Authenticate a user and return an access token.

Verifies the provided credentials against the active database. If valid, a JWT token is generated for subsequent requests.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default username str

The login identifier.

required password str

The secret password for the user.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description dict dict

containing the access_token and token_type.

Raises:

Type Description HTTPException
  • 401: If credentials are not valid.

docs.title: Inventory API Endpoints
docs.text: Provides endpoints for managing physical inventory and system status across multiple warehouse facilities.

docs.title: get_inventory_status() async
docs.text:
Retrieve the current operational status of the inventory system.

This endpoint performs a heartbeat check on the warehouse management subsystem and returns a summary of active nodes.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: None

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: dict A dictionary containing: - status (str): System health ("operational", "degraded", "offline"). - active_warehouses (int): Count of connected warehouse facilities. - last_sync (str): ISO timestamp of the last database sync.

docs.title: map_inventory_location(sku, aisle, shelf, bin_num) async
docs.text:
Map an inventory item to a specific physical warehouse location.

This endpoint heavily utilizes the nested WarehouseLocation model.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default sku str

The unique stock keeping unit identifier.

required aisle str

The warehouse aisle.

required shelf str

The shelf level.

required bin_num str

The specific storage bin.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description InventoryItem

The updated item with its mapped physical location.

docs.title: register_organization(name, account_type, emails) async
docs.text:
Register a new Organization.

This endpoint initializes a new tenant space for enterprise customers.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

The legal name of the organization.

required account_type AccountType

The subscription tier (Basic, Premium, Enterprise).

required emails List[str]

List of administrative contact email addresses.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description Organization

The newly created organization record.

docs.title: create_user(user_data) async
docs.text:
Create a new user within the system.

Accepts username and email payload to provision a new active user.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default user_data UserCreate

The incoming JSON payload representing setup parameters.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description User User

The created User instance containing the assigned ID.

docs.title: delete_user(user_id) async
docs.text:
Deactivate a user account.

This is a soft-delete operation that updates the 'is_active' flag.

docs.title: get_user(user_id) async
docs.text:
Retrieve a user from the database by their unique ID.

This endpoint queries the active database for a user matching the provided ID. If the user has been deactivated or does not exist, a 404 is returned.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default user_id int

The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description User User

The user object containing profile details.

Raises:

Type Description HTTPException
  • 404: If the user does not exist.

docs.title: app.ml
docs.text: Machine Learning module.

docs.title: Advanced ML Processing & Modeling
docs.text: This package contains high-performance weather prediction models. By refactoring this entire module, we are forcing AutoAPI to regenerate the index and diagrams.

docs.title: GlobalWeatherNet(hparams, model_name='GlobalNet-V2')
docs.text:
A deep learning model architecture for global-scale weather forecasting.

This class replaces the previous WeatherPredictor to test if AutoAPI correctly re-indexes the new class name and suppresses the old one.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: hparams : HyperParameters The hyperparameter configuration for the network. model_name : str, default="GlobalNet-V2" Identifier for the model instance.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: version : str The version string for the model logic.

docs.title: export_onnx(path)
docs.text: Exports the current model state to the ONNX format.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: path : str File system path where the .onnx file will be saved.

docs.title: train_step(data)
docs.text: Executes a single training iteration.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: data : Dict[str, Any] A dictionary containing 'features' and 'targets' tensors.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: float The loss value after the optimization step.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: This method uses a mock stochastic gradient descent update. It is intended for testing AutoAPI's ability to render 'Notes' blocks.

docs.title: Warns
docs.text: UserWarning If the batch size in data differs from hparams.batch_size.

docs.title: HyperParameters(epochs=100, batch_size=64, layers=None) dataclass
docs.text: Configuration for model training.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: epochs : int Number of full passes through the dataset. batch_size : int Number of samples per gradient update. layers : List[int] Number of neurons in each hidden layer.

docs.text: This module demonstrates how Sphinx handles multiple files within the same package (ml/processing.py).

docs.title: Returns

docs.title: Cross-Repository Data Flow
docs.text: Auto-generated pipeline visualization showing how the three neural-lam ecosystem repositories interact. Last updated: 2026-03-11 18:34:25

docs.title: The Three-Repo Pipeline
docs.text:
The neural-lam ecosystem consists of three interconnected repositories that form a complete weather prediction pipeline:

Repository Purpose Output mllam-data-prep Data preprocessing .zarr datasets weather-model-graphs Graph topology .pt graph files neural-lam Model training & eval Trained weights

docs.title: Data Flow Diagram
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph mllam_data_prep ["📦 mllam-data-prep"]
        MDP_CONFIG["config.yaml\n(data sources, variables)"]
        MDP_PROCESS["Data Processing\n(xarray/dask)"]
        MDP_OUTPUT[".zarr Dataset\n(standardized weather data)"]
        MDP_CONFIG --> MDP_PROCESS --> MDP_OUTPUT
    end

    subgraph weather_model_graphs ["🌐 weather-model-graphs"]
        WMG_CONFIG["graph_config.yaml\n(mesh topology)"]
        WMG_BUILD["Graph Builder\n(networkx)"]
        WMG_OUTPUT[".pt Graph Files\n(mesh, g2m, m2g edges)"]
        WMG_CONFIG --> WMG_BUILD --> WMG_OUTPUT
    end

    subgraph neural_lam ["🧠 neural-lam"]
        NL_DATASTORE["MDPDatastore\n(reads .zarr)"]
        NL_GRAPH["Graph Loading\n(reads .pt files)"]
        NL_MODEL["GNN Model\n(Hi-LAM / GraphLAM)"]
        NL_TRAIN["Training Loop\n(PyTorch Lightning)"]
        NL_EVAL["Evaluation\n(RMSE, MAE, ACC)"]

        NL_DATASTORE --> NL_MODEL
        NL_GRAPH --> NL_MODEL
        NL_MODEL --> NL_TRAIN
        NL_TRAIN --> NL_EVAL
    end

    MDP_OUTPUT -->|".zarr via\nMDPDatastore"| NL_DATASTORE
    WMG_OUTPUT -->|".pt graph\nfiles"| NL_GRAPH

    style mllam_data_prep fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px
    style weather_model_graphs fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    style neural_lam fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    style MDP_OUTPUT fill:#bbdefb,stroke:#1565c0
    style WMG_OUTPUT fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style NL_MODEL fill:#ffe0b2,stroke:#ef6c00
    style NL_EVAL fill:#ffccbc,stroke:#bf360c

docs.title: 1. mllam-data-prep → .zarr Datasets
docs.text:
The mllam-data-prep repository processes raw weather data (e.g., ERA5, DANRA) into standardized .zarr datasets. It:

  • Reads configuration from config.yaml defining data sources and variables
  • Uses xarray and dask for parallel data processing
  • Outputs .zarr stores with consistent coordinate systems and variable naming

docs.title: 2. weather-model-graphs → .pt Graph Files
docs.text:
The weather-model-graphs repository generates the graph structures used by the neural-lam GNN models:

  • Mesh graphs: Define the internal model resolution hierarchy
  • Grid-to-Mesh (g2m): Map input grid points to mesh nodes
  • Mesh-to-Grid (m2g): Map mesh predictions back to output grid

docs.title: 3. neural-lam — Core Training Engine
docs.text:
The neural-lam repository is the core training engine that:

  • Loads .zarr data via MDPDatastore (from mllam-data-prep output)
  • Loads .pt graph files (from weather-model-graphs output)
  • Trains Graph Neural Network models (Hi-LAM, GraphLAM)
  • Evaluates predictions using RMSE, MAE, and ACC metrics

docs.title: Integration Points
docs.text:
graph TD
    A["mllam-data-prep\nconfig.yaml"] -->|"creates"| B[".zarr store"]
    C["weather-model-graphs\ngraph_config.yaml"] -->|"creates"| D[".pt files"]
    B -->|"imported via"| E["neural_lam.weather_dataset\nMDPDatastore"]
    D -->|"loaded in"| F["neural_lam.models\ngraph loading"]
    E --> G["Training Pipeline"]
    F --> G
    G --> H["Evaluation Metrics"]
    H --> I["Trained Model Weights"]

    style A fill:#e3f2fd
    style C fill:#e8f5e9
    style G fill:#fff3e0
    style I fill:#ffccbc

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The ER diagram is generated by generate_diagrams.py:

  1. Model Discovery: Finds all classes inheriting from pydantic.BaseModel

  3. Relationship Detection: Checks if any field's type annotation references another model
  4. Mermaid Generation: Converts entities and relationships into a Mermaid erDiagram

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The diagram above is generated automatically by the generate_diagrams.py script:

  1. Module Discovery: Walks the backend/app/ directory tree and categorizes files by layer
  2. Import Analysis: Parses each .py file's AST to find from ... import ... statements
  3. Connection Mapping: Maps imports to source→target connections between layers
  4. Mermaid Generation: Converts the graph into a Mermaid.js flowchart

No manual editing required. Just run:

python scripts/generate_diagrams.py

docs.title: Inventory API Endpoints
docs.text: Provides endpoints for managing physical inventory and system status across multiple warehouse facilities.

docs.title: get_inventory_status() async
docs.text:
Retrieve the current operational status of the inventory system.

This endpoint performs a heartbeat check on the warehouse management subsystem and returns a summary of active nodes.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: None

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: dict A dictionary containing: - status (str): System health ("operational", "degraded", "offline"). - active_warehouses (int): Count of connected warehouse facilities. - last_sync (str): ISO timestamp of the last database sync.

docs.title: map_inventory_location(sku, aisle, shelf, bin_num) async
docs.text:
Map an inventory item to a specific physical warehouse location.

This endpoint heavily utilizes the nested WarehouseLocation model.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default sku str

The unique stock keeping unit identifier.

required aisle str

The warehouse aisle.

required shelf str

The shelf level.

required bin_num str

The specific storage bin.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description InventoryItem

The updated item with its mapped physical location.

docs.title: Entity: Token
docs.text:
Authentication token details.

Field Type Description Default access_token str The JWT access token. Required token_type str The type of token (e.g., 'Bearer'). Required expires_in int The duration in seconds until the token expires. 3600

docs.title: Entity: Notification
docs.text:
Notification model for system alerts and user messages.

Field Type Description Default id str Unique identifier for the notification. Required user_id int The ID of the user receiving the notification. Required type Literal The delivery channel. Required message str The actual notification content. Required read bool Whether the notification has been read. False

docs.title: Entity: NotificationCreate
docs.text:
Payload to trigger a new notification.

Field Type Description Default user_id int The ID of the target user. Required type Literal The desired delivery channel. Required message str The message payload. Required

docs.title: Entity: BaseEntity
docs.text:
Base class for all enterprise entities.

Field Type Description Default id str Unique identifier. Required created_at str Creation ISO timestamp. Required metadata Optional Extensible metadata dictionary. None

docs.title: Entity: Organization
docs.text:
An enterprise organization.

Field Type Description Default id str Unique identifier. Required created_at str Creation ISO timestamp. Required metadata Optional Extensible metadata dictionary. None name str Name of the organization. Required account_type AccountType The subscription tier. AccountType.BASIC parent_org_id Optional Parent organization ID if applicable (forward reference test). None contact_emails List List of administrative contact emails. Required

docs.title: Entity: Invoice
docs.text:
A billing invoice for an organization.

Field Type Description Default invoice_id str Unique invoice number. Required organization Organization The organization being billed. Required items List List of line items on the invoice. Required total_amount float Total amount due. Required is_paid bool Whether the invoice has been settled. False

docs.title: Entity: InvoiceItem
docs.text:
Line item on an enterprise invoice.

Field Type Description Default description str Line item description. Required quantity int Quantity of items. 1 unit_price float Price per unit. Required

docs.title: Entity: InventoryItem
docs.text:
An item stored in the warehouse.

Field Type Description Default sku str Stock Keeping Unit. <lambda>() name str Product name. Required status Literal Current stock status. available location Optional Where the item is physically located. None weight_kg Union Weight of the item in kilograms (testing Union). Required

docs.title: Entity: WarehouseLocation
docs.text:
A physical storage location.

Field Type Description Default aisle str Aisle identifier. Required shelf str Shelf level. Required bin str Specific bin number. Required

docs.title: Entity: Product
docs.text:
Simplified Product model.
Field Type Description Default id int Product ID. Required name str Display name. Required price float Price in USD. Required

docs.title: Entity: User
docs.text:
Simplified User model for ER diagram stress testing.

We have removed the nested Address model and other metadata 
to see if the ER diagram shrinks and simplifies automatically.
Field Type Description Default id int Unique identifier. Required username str Login name. Required email str Primary contact email. Required

docs.title: Contributing Guide
docs.text: Thank you for considering contributing to AutoCodeDoc! This guide covers docstring standards, development workflow, and how to submit your changes.

docs.title: Docstring Standard: NumPy Style
docs.text: All code in this project must use NumPy-style docstrings. This ensures consistent, machine-readable documentation that Sphinx can render automatically.

docs.title: Required Sections
docs.text:
Every public function, class, and method must include:

Section Required? Description Summary ✅ Yes One-line description of what it does Parameters ✅ Yes All parameters with types and descriptions Returns ✅ Yes Return value with type and description Raises If applicable Exceptions that can be raised Examples Encouraged Usage examples with expected output Notes Optional Implementation notes or caveats See Also Optional Links to related functions/classes

docs.title: Template
docs.text:
def process_data(input_data: dict, validate: bool = True) -> dict:
    """
    Process incoming data through the transformation pipeline.

    Takes raw input data, applies validation and normalization steps,
    and returns the processed result ready for downstream consumption.

    Parameters
    ----------
    input_data : dict
        The raw data dictionary to process. Must contain at minimum
        a ``"type"`` key indicating the data category.
    validate : bool, optional
        Whether to run validation checks before processing.
        Default is True.

    Returns
    -------
    dict
        The processed data with normalized keys and validated values.

    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If ``input_data`` is empty or missing required keys.
    TypeError
        If ``input_data`` is not a dictionary.

    Examples
    --------
    >>> result = process_data({"type": "user", "name": "Alice"})
    >>> print(result["status"])
    'processed'

    See Also
    --------
    validate_data : Validation step used internally.
    normalize_keys : Key normalization utility.
    """
    ...

docs.title: Pydantic Model Docstrings
docs.text:
For Pydantic models, use both class docstrings and Field(description=...):

class UserCreate(BaseModel):
    """
    Model used to create a new user account.

    Attributes
    ----------
    username : str
        The desired username (3-50 characters).
    email : str
        A valid email address for the account.
    bio : str, optional
        A short biography for the user profile.
    """

    username: str = Field(description="The desired username (3-50 characters).")
    email: str = Field(description="A valid email address for the account.")
    bio: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="A short biography for the user profile.")

docs.title: 1. Fork and Clone
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/AutoCodeDoc.git
cd AutoCodeDoc/doc_automation_platform
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # or venv\Scripts\activate on Windows
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 2. Create a Feature Branch
docs.text: git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

docs.title: 3. Make Changes
docs.text:
• Write code with NumPy-style docstrings
  • Add Field(description=...) to all Pydantic fields
  • Keep functions focused and well-documented

docs.title: 4. Check Coverage Before Committing
docs.text:
# Check docstring coverage
interrogate backend/ -v --fail-under 90

# Run the full pipeline
python run_pipeline.py --build-only

docs.title: 5. Submit a Pull Request
docs.text:
• Ensure all docstring coverage checks pass
  • Verify Sphinx builds without warnings
  • Include a clear description of your changes
  • Reference any related issues

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text: Rule Standard Docstring format NumPy-style Type hints Required on all public functions Line length 100 characters max Imports Sorted with isort Formatting black with default settings

docs.title: CI/CD Pipeline
docs.text:
When you push to main or open a PR, the CI pipeline automatically:

  1. Lints the codebase for syntax errors
  2. Checks docstring coverage with interrogate (non-blocking — warns but doesn't fail)
  3. Builds Sphinx documentation and checks for warnings
  4. Deploys to GitHub Pages (on merge to main)

```{admonition} Important :class: warning

The docstring coverage check is non-blocking by design. It will comment on your PR if coverage drops, but it won't prevent merging. This is intentional — we want to track coverage trends without blocking development. ```

docs.title: Adding New Modules
docs.text:
When adding a new module to the backend:

  1. Create the file in the appropriate directory (api/, services/, models/, utils/)
  2. Add an __init__.py if creating a new package
  3. Write complete NumPy-style docstrings for all public items
  4. Run python run_pipeline.py --build-only to verify it appears in the API Reference
  5. The ER diagram and system architecture will update automatically

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text:
• Open a GitHub Issue
  • Check the Usage Guide for workflow details
  • Review the API Reference for existing patterns

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before installing the AutoCodeDoc Platform, ensure you have:

  • Python 3.10+ — Download Python
  • pip — Comes bundled with Python 3.4+
  • Git — Download Git
  • Graphviz (optional, for SVG diagram export) — Download Graphviz

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/koffandaff/AutoCodeDoc.git
cd AutoCodeDoc/doc_automation_platform

docs.title: 2. Create a Virtual Environment
docs.text:
# Create
python -m venv venv

# Activate (Windows)
venv\Scripts\activate

# Activate (macOS/Linux)
source venv/bin/activate

docs.title: 3. Install Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt

This installs everything needed for both documentation engines:

Package Purpose sphinx Primary documentation engine sphinx-autoapi Auto-generates API docs from source code sphinxcontrib-mermaid Renders Mermaid.js diagrams in Sphinx sphinx-design Cards, grids, tabs for rich layouts interrogate Docstring coverage analysis mkdocs-material Secondary MkDocs site (legacy) mkdocstrings MkDocs Python doc renderer

docs.title: 4. Verify Installation
docs.text:
# Check Sphinx
sphinx-build --version

# Check interrogate
interrogate --version

# Check all imports work
python -c "import sphinx, autoapi, sphinxcontrib.mermaid; print('All good!')"

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Variable Default Description PYTHONPATH ./backend Required for autoapi module discovery DOCS_OUTPUT ./site Where built docs are written

Set these in your shell or .env file:

export PYTHONPATH="$PWD/backend"
export DOCS_OUTPUT="$PWD/site"

docs.title: IDE Integration
docs.text:
For VS Code, add to .vscode/settings.json:

{
    "python.analysis.extraPaths": ["./backend"],
    "python.formatting.provider": "black",
    "restructuredtext.confPath": "${workspaceFolder}/doc_automation_platform/docs/sphinx"
}

docs.title: Single Command (Recommended)
docs.text:
python run_pipeline.py

This runs the full pipeline: lint → coverage check → diagram generation → Sphinx build → optional serve.

docs.title: Manual Build
docs.text:
# Sphinx only
cd docs/sphinx
sphinx-build -b html . ../../site/sphinx

# MkDocs only
mkdocs build -d site

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
```{admonition} Common Issues :class: tip

  • ModuleNotFoundError: Ensure PYTHONPATH includes the backend/ directory
  • Mermaid not rendering: Check that sphinxcontrib-mermaid is installed
  • AutoAPI shows no modules: Verify autoapi_dirs in conf.py points to valid Python packages ```

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Read the Usage Guide for an end-to-end walkthrough
  • Explore the Architecture Diagrams
  • Browse the API Reference

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This guide walks you through the full documentation workflow — from writing docstrings to deploying a production-ready documentation site.

docs.title: End-to-End Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A["Write Code + Docstrings"] --> B["Run Pipeline"]
    B --> C["Diagrams Generated"]
    B --> D["Coverage Checked"]
    B --> E["Sphinx Build"]
    E --> F["Deploy to GitHub Pages"]
    D --> G["docs_report.json"]
    style A fill:#4051b5,color:#fff
    style F fill:#4caf50,color:#fff
    style G fill:#ff9800,color:#fff

docs.title: Step 1: Write NumPy-Style Docstrings
docs.text: All documentation is extracted from your source code. Use NumPy-style docstrings throughout:

docs.title: For Functions
docs.text:
async def fetch_user_by_id(user_id: int) -> Optional[User]:
    """
    Retrieve a user from the database by their unique ID.

    Parameters
    ----------
    user_id : int
        The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.

    Returns
    -------
    Optional[User]
        The user object if found, otherwise None.

    Raises
    ------
    HTTPException
        If the user does not exist (404).

    Examples
    --------
    >>> user = await fetch_user_by_id(42)
    >>> print(user.username)
    'johndoe'
    """
    return await db.get(user_id)

docs.title: For Pydantic Models
docs.text:
class User(BaseModel):
    """
    User model representing an account in the system.

    Attributes
    ----------
    id : int
        The unique identifier for the user.
    username : str
        The login username.
    email : str
        The email address of the user.
    """

    id: int = Field(description="The unique identifier for the user.")
    username: str = Field(description="The login username.")
    email: str = Field(description="The email address of the user.")

docs.title: Step 2: Run the Pipeline
docs.text:
# Full pipeline (lint → coverage → diagrams → build → serve)
python run_pipeline.py

# Build only (no server)
python run_pipeline.py --build-only

# Skip coverage check (faster iteration)
python run_pipeline.py --skip-coverage

docs.title: What Happens
docs.text:
1. Lint Check — Validates Python syntax across the backend
  2. Interrogate Coverage — Reports docstring coverage percentage (non-blocking)
  3. Diagram Generation — AST-parses your code to produce Mermaid diagrams
  4. Sphinx Build — Compiles all docs into a static HTML site
  5. Report Generation — Creates docs_report.json with build metadata

docs.title: Step 3: Review the Output
docs.text:
After the pipeline completes, you'll find:

Output Location Description Sphinx HTML site site/sphinx/ Primary documentation site MkDocs HTML site site/ Legacy MkDocs documentation Build report docs_report.json Modules, coverage, diagrams, warnings API reference site/sphinx/api/ Auto-generated from source

docs.title: Step 4: Explore the Live Site
docs.text:
Open your browser to view the documentation:

# Sphinx (primary) — opens automatically with run_pipeline.py
http://127.0.0.1:8002

# MkDocs (secondary)
http://127.0.0.1:8001

docs.title: Adding a New API Endpoint
docs.text:
1. Create the endpoint in backend/app/api/
  2. Add NumPy-style docstrings with Parameters, Returns, Raises
  3. Run python run_pipeline.py --build-only
  4. Verify the endpoint appears in the API Reference

docs.title: Adding a New Pydantic Model
docs.text:
1. Define the model in backend/app/models/
  2. Add Field(description="...") to every field
  3. Run the pipeline — the ER diagram and schema pages update automatically

docs.title: Checking Docstring Coverage
docs.text:
# Quick check
interrogate backend/ -v

# With badge generation
interrogate backend/ -v --generate-badge docs/sphinx/_static/

docs.title: Architecture of the Doc System
docs.text:
graph TD
    subgraph Sources["Source Code"]
        PY["Python Modules"]
        DS["NumPy Docstrings"]
        PM["Pydantic Models"]
    end

    subgraph Engine["Documentation Engine"]
        AA["sphinx-autoapi"]
        NAP["napoleon"]
        MER["sphinxcontrib-mermaid"]
        GEN["generate_diagrams.py"]
    end

    subgraph Output["Generated Docs"]
        API["API Reference"]
        ARCH["Architecture Diagrams"]
        ER["ER Diagrams"]
        FLOW["Data Flow"]
    end

    PY --> AA
    DS --> NAP
    PM --> GEN
    PY --> GEN
    AA --> API
    NAP --> API
    MER --> ARCH
    GEN --> ER
    GEN --> FLOW

    style Sources fill:#e8eaf6
    style Engine fill:#fff3e0
    style Output fill:#e8f5e9
